getting-started.name: Getting Started
getting-started.description: Learn to make the cat move and talk

narration.default: Drag the move 10 steps block from the Motion category into the workspace.
Now drag the say hello for 2 seconds block from the Looks category and snap it below the move block.

steps.title: Click the green flag to start

narration.default: Finally, drag the when green flag clicked block from Events and place it on top of your blocks. Then click the green flag to run your project!

getting-started-asl.name: Getting Started - ASL
getting-started-asl.description: Getting started with Scratch using American Sign Language

add-sprite.name: Add a Sprite
add-sprite.description: Learn how to add a new sprite

narration.default: To add a new sprite, click the Choose a Sprite button at the bottom right of the screen. Pick any sprite you like!

add-a-backdrop.name: Add a Backdrop
add-a-backdrop.description: Learn how to add a backdrop

narration.default: Click the Choose a Backdrop button at the bottom right of the screen to add a new backdrop to your project.

switch-costume.name: Animate a Sprite
switch-costume.description: Learn how to animate a sprite by switching costumes

narration.default: Use the switch costume block inside a forever loop to animate your sprite. Each costume is like a frame of animation!

move-around-with-arrow-keys.name: Use Arrow Keys
move-around-with-arrow-keys.description: Learn how to move a sprite with arrow keys

narration.default: Use when key pressed blocks for the left and right arrow keys. Add change x blocks to move the sprite left and right.
Now add when key pressed blocks for the up and down arrow keys. Use change y blocks to move the sprite up and down.

change-size.name: Change Size
change-size.description: Learn how to change the size of a sprite

narration.default: Use the change size block to make your sprite grow or shrink. Put it in a forever loop to keep changing!

glide-around.name: Glide Around
glide-around.description: Learn how to make a sprite glide around the stage

narration.default: Use the glide block to make your sprite move smoothly back and forth across the stage.
Use the glide to x y block to make your sprite glide to a specific point on the stage.

spin-video.name: Make It Spin
spin-video.description: Learn how to make a sprite spin

narration.default: Use the turn block inside a forever loop to make your sprite spin continuously.
Use the point in direction block to set which way your sprite faces before spinning.

record-a-sound.name: Record a Sound
record-a-sound.description: Learn how to record your own sounds

narration.default: Click on the Sounds tab at the top of the screen to open the sound editor.
Click the Record button to start recording a new sound.
Press the record button and speak into your microphone to record your sound.
After recording, you can trim and edit your sound. Press Save to keep it.
Now use the play sound block to play your recorded sound in your project!

hide-and-show.name: Hide and Show
hide-and-show.description: Learn how to hide and show sprites

narration.default: Use the hide and show blocks to make your sprite disappear and reappear. Add a wait block in between for a timed effect!

animate-a-name.name: Animate a Name
animate-a-name.description: Make letters spin, grow, and change color

narration.default: Click the Choose a Sprite button and pick a letter sprite to start spelling out a name.
Add a when this sprite clicked block and a play sound block so the letter plays a sound when you click it.
Choose another letter sprite to add the next letter of your name.
Add a change color effect block to make this letter change color when clicked.

steps.title: Pick Another Letter Sprite & Make It Spin

narration.default: Pick another letter and add a turn block inside a forever loop to make it spin.

steps.title: Pick Another Letter Sprite & Make It Grow

narration.default: Pick one more letter and add a change size block to make it grow when clicked.

Animate-A-Character.name: Animate A Character
Animate-A-Character.description: Add a backdrop, sprite, sound, and movement to create an animated character

narration.default: Click the Choose a Backdrop button and pick a backdrop for your scene.
Click the Choose a Sprite button and pick a character for your animation.
Add a say block to make your character say something when the green flag is clicked.
Add a play sound block to give your character a sound effect.
Use the switch costume block to animate your character talking by switching between costumes.
Add when key pressed blocks with change x blocks so your character can move left and right with the arrow keys.
Use change y blocks to make your character jump up and come back down.
Add a change color effect block to make your character change colors.

Tell-A-Story.name: Create A Story
Tell-A-Story.description: Create a story with characters, dialogue, and scenes

narration.default: Click the Choose a Backdrop button and pick a backdrop for your story scene.
Click the Choose a Sprite button and pick a character for your story.
Add a say block to make your character say something as part of the story.
Add another character sprite so two characters can have a conversation.
Flip the direction of the second character so the two characters face each other.
Use say and wait blocks on both characters with different timing to create a conversation.
Add another backdrop for a new scene in your story.
Use the switch backdrop block to change scenes in your story.
Use the hide block to make a character disappear when the scene changes.
Use the show block to make a character appear in the new scene.

say-it-out-loud.name: Create Animations That Talk
say-it-out-loud.description: Use the Text to Speech extension to make characters speak out loud

steps.title: Add the Text to Speech blocks

narration.default: Click the Add Extension button at the bottom left and choose the Text to Speech extension to add talking blocks.
Drag the speak block into the workspace and type what you want your character to say out loud.
Use the set voice block to choose a different voice for your character.
Add motion blocks to make your character move while it talks.
Click the Choose a Backdrop button and pick a backdrop for your talking animation.
Add another sprite to create a conversation between two characters.
Use the speak block with different words and set voice blocks to make your character sing a song.
Add a change color effect block to make your character change color while speaking.
Add a turn block to make your character spin around while it talks.
Use the change size block to make your character grow and shrink while speaking.

imagine.name: Imagine a World
imagine.description: Create an interactive world with multiple characters and backdrops

steps.title: Type What You Want to Say

narration.default: Click on the say block and type what you want your character to say.

steps.title: Click the Green Flag to Start

narration.default: Click the green flag to see your character say the message you typed.
Click the Choose a Backdrop button and pick any backdrop to set the scene for your world.
Click the Choose a Sprite button and pick any sprite to add to your world.

steps.title: Press the Space Key to Glide

narration.default: Add a when space key pressed block with a glide block to make your sprite fly around when you press space.
Add another sprite to your world to make it more interesting.
Use the change x block with when key pressed to move this sprite left and right.
Use the change y block with when key pressed to move this sprite up and down.
Add a next costume block to make your sprite animate by switching between costumes.
Use the glide to x y block to make your sprite glide to a specific location.
Use the change size block to make your sprite grow and shrink.
Add another backdrop to create a new scene in your world.
Use the switch backdrop block to change between different scenes.
Click the Sounds tab and add or record a sound to play in your world.
Choose a sound from the library and add a play sound block to your code.

add-effects.name: Add Effects
add-effects.description: Add visual effects like color, fisheye, and more

narration.default: Use the change effect block to add visual effects like color, fisheye, whirl, pixelate, mosaic, brightness, and ghost to your sprite.

make-it-fly.name: Make it Fly
make-it-fly.description: Create a flying game with scoring and scenery

narration.default: Click the Choose a Backdrop button and pick a sky background for your flying game.
Choose a character sprite that will fly around the screen.
Add a say block to make your flying character say something when the game starts.
Add when key pressed blocks with change x and change y blocks to control your character with arrow keys.
Add another sprite that your character will try to collect as it flies around.
Add code to make the collectible object move across the screen and reappear.
Click on your flying character sprite to select it and add more code.
Create a variable called score to keep track of how many objects you collect.
Add a change score by 1 block inside an if touching block so the score goes up when you collect an object.
Add a scenery sprite like clouds or buildings to make the background more interesting.
Add code to make the scenery move across the screen to create a scrolling effect.
Use the next costume block to change the scenery appearance as it moves.

Make-Music.name: Make Music
Make-Music.description: Create a musical instrument project with sounds and beats

narration.default: Click the Choose a Sprite button and pick an instrument sprite to use in your music project.
Add a when this sprite clicked block and a play sound block so the instrument plays when you click it.
Add multiple play sound blocks with different notes to create a song that plays automatically.

steps.title: Choose a Drum & Make a Beat

narration.default: Pick a drum sprite and add play drum blocks to create a beat.

steps.title: Choose the Microphone Sprite & Surprise Beatbox

narration.default: Pick the microphone sprite and add random play sound blocks for a surprise beatbox effect.

pong.name: Pong Game
pong.description: Create a classic Pong game with a ball, paddle, and score

narration.default: Click the Choose a Backdrop button and pick a simple backdrop for your Pong game.
Add a ball sprite that will bounce around the stage.
Add code to make the ball move and bounce off the edges of the stage.
Add a paddle sprite at the bottom of the stage.
Add code to make the paddle follow your mouse pointer left and right.
Click on the ball sprite to select it and add more code.

steps.title: Add Code to Bounce the Ball Off the Paddle

narration.default: Add an if touching paddle block to make the ball bounce off the paddle.
Create a variable called score to track how many times you hit the ball.
Select the score variable from the dropdown menu in the change block.
Add the change score by 1 block inside the if touching paddle section so the score goes up each time you hit the ball.
Add a set score to 0 block at the start so the score resets when you begin a new game.
Add a line sprite at the bottom of the stage to mark the game over zone.
Add code to stop the game when the ball touches the line at the bottom.

Make-A-Game.name: Make a Clicker Game
Make-A-Game.description: Create a clicker game where you click sprites to score points

narration.default: Click the Choose a Sprite button and pick a sprite to be your clickable target.
Add a when this sprite clicked block and a play sound block so the sprite makes a sound when you click it.
Create a variable called score to track how many times you click the sprite.
Add a change score by 1 block inside the when this sprite clicked section to increase the score each time.
Add a go to random position block so the sprite moves to a new spot after each click.
Add a change color effect block to make the sprite change color each time it is clicked.
Add a set score to 0 block under when green flag clicked so the score resets at the start.

Chase-Game.name: Make a Chase Game
Chase-Game.description: Create a chase game with two sprites and scoring

narration.default: Click the Choose a Backdrop button and pick an underwater or outdoor backdrop for your chase game.
Add a sprite that you will control to chase after objects.

steps.title: Move Right & Left With Arrow Keys

narration.default: Add when key pressed blocks for right and left arrow keys with change x blocks to move the sprite.

steps.title: Move Up & Down With Arrow Keys

narration.default: Add when key pressed blocks for up and down arrow keys with change y blocks to move the sprite.
Add another sprite that will move randomly for your character to chase.
Add a glide to random position block inside a forever loop to make this sprite move randomly.

steps.title: In Octopus Sprite, When Touching Play Sound

narration.default: Select your character sprite and add an if touching block with a play sound block inside it.
Create a variable called score to track how many times you catch the other sprite.

steps.title: In Octopus Sprite, When Touching Add Score

narration.default: Add a change score by 1 block inside the if touching section so the score goes up each time you catch the sprite.

code-cartoon.name: Code a Cartoon
code-cartoon.description: Animate characters in a pre-made cartoon project

steps.title: Say Something When You Click the Green Flag

narration.default: Add a when green flag clicked block and a say block to make a character say something when the project starts.

steps.title: Animate a Character When You Click It

narration.default: Add a when this sprite clicked block with switch costume blocks to animate a character when you click on it.
Click on a different character sprite in the sprite list to select it.

steps.title: Use a Minus Sign to Get Smaller

narration.default: Use a minus number in the change size block to make a character shrink when clicked.

steps.title: Make a Character Grow and Shrink

narration.default: Add change size blocks with positive and negative numbers in a loop to make a character grow and shrink.
Click on another character sprite in the sprite list to add code to it.
Use change y blocks with positive and negative numbers to make the character jump up and come back down.

steps.title: Click a Character to Change Scenes

narration.default: Add a when this sprite clicked block with a switch backdrop block to change scenes when you click a character.
Add a glide block to make a character glide smoothly across the stage.
Use the next costume block in a loop to animate a character by cycling through its costumes.

steps.title: Choose More Characters to Add to Your Cartoon

narration.default: Click the Choose a Sprite button to add more characters and keep building your cartoon!

cartoon-network.name: Animate an Adventure Game
cartoon-network.description: Create an adventure game with characters, collecting, and scoring

narration.default: Pick a character sprite and add a show block so it appears on screen.
Add a say block to make your character introduce themselves or give instructions.
Add a glide block to make your character move smoothly across the stage.
Add another sprite that your character will chase and collect.
Add code to detect when your character touches the object and collect it.
Create a variable called score to keep track of how many objects you collect.
Add a change score by 1 block so the score increases each time you collect an object.
Add code to switch to a new backdrop when the score reaches a certain number to level up.

Video-Sensing.name: Video Sensing
Video-Sensing.description: Use your camera to interact with sprites

narration.default: Click the Add Extension button at the bottom left and choose the Video Sensing extension.
Add a when video motion is greater than block to detect when you wave your hand near the cat sprite.
Add a next costume block so the sprite animates when it detects video motion.
Add a balloon sprite and use video sensing to pop it when you wave your hand over it.

talking.name: Talking Tales
talking.description: Create a multi-scene talking tale with music and dance

steps.title: Click to Add the Text-to-Speech Blocks

narration.default: Click the Add Extension button and choose the Text to Speech extension to add talking blocks.
Click the Choose a Sprite button and pick a character for your tale.
Add a speak block and type what you want the character to say out loud.
Click the Choose a Backdrop button and pick a backdrop for your first scene.

steps.title: Click a Character to Go to the Next Backdrop

narration.default: Add a when this sprite clicked block with a next backdrop block to change scenes when you click the character.
Add another character sprite to your tale.
Add motion blocks to make this character move around the stage.
Add another backdrop for a new scene in your tale.
Use switch costume blocks to animate the character's mouth moving while it speaks.
Add a third backdrop for the final scene of your tale.

steps.title: Choose a Song to Dance To

narration.default: Go to the Sounds tab and add a music track from the sound library for your characters to dance to.
Add turn and move blocks to make your character dance when the music plays.

steps.title: Get the Ask and Answer Blocks from the Sensing Category

narration.default: Use the ask block from the Sensing category to let the user interact with your tale by typing an answer.

blink-led.name: Blink an LED
blink-led.description: Learn to blink an LED using Arduino blocks and the simulator

button-led.name: Control an LED with a Button
button-led.description: Turn an LED on when a button is pressed and off when released

led-glow-effect.name: LED Glow Effect
led-glow-effect.description: Use PWM to fade an LED smoothly brighter and dimmer

traffic-light.name: Traffic Light
traffic-light.description: Build a working traffic light with red, yellow and green LEDs

sos-morse-code.name: SOS Morse Code
sos-morse-code.description: Blink an LED with the SOS Morse signal — three short, three long, three short

night-lamp.name: Night Lamp
night-lamp.description: Turn an LED on automatically when the room gets dark, off when it's bright

security-alarm.name: Security Alarm
security-alarm.description: Set off an alarm when a motion sensor spots someone sneaking past

fan-speed-control.name: Fan Speed Control
fan-speed-control.description: Turn a knob to speed up or slow down a fan — smoothly, just like a real dimmer

move-a-servo.name: Move a Servo
move-a-servo.description: Send a servo motor to a specific angle and watch it sweep — 0, 90, 180 degrees on cue

automatic-plant-irrigation.name: Automatic Plant Irrigation
automatic-plant-irrigation.description: Water a plant automatically when the soil gets too dry

car-parking-sensor.name: Car Parking Sensor System
car-parking-sensor.description: Beep faster as a car gets closer to the wall — just like a real parking sensor

automatic-hand-sanitizer.name: Automatic Hand Sanitizer
automatic-hand-sanitizer.description: Squirt sanitizer automatically when a hand comes near — no touching needed

smart-door-lock.name: Smart Door Lock
smart-door-lock.description: Unlock a door with a secret keypad code — wrong code, door stays shut

smart-people-counter.name: Smart People Counter
smart-people-counter.description: Count everyone who walks past with an IR sensor — just like a shop door

line-following-robot.name: Line Following Robot
line-following-robot.description: Build a robot that drives along a black line on its own — IR sensors see, motors steer

smart-thermostat.name: Smart Thermostat
smart-thermostat.description: Read the room temperature and switch a heater or fan on and off to keep things just right

petrol-pump-meter.name: Petrol Pump Meter
petrol-pump-meter.description: Measure fuel flowing through a pipe and show the amount on a display — like at the petrol station

html-hello-web.name: Hello Web 🌐
html-hello-web.description: Build your very first webpage and see your content appear.

html-headings.name: Headings — Big to Small 📏
html-headings.description: Add headings and see how they change in size.

html-paragraphs.name: Write Your Story ✍️
html-paragraphs.description: Add real text to your page in clean, readable paragraphs.

html-text-formatting.name: Text Formatting ✨
html-text-formatting.description: Make text bold, italic, and underlined with inline tags.

html-links.name: Create Links and Navigation 🔗
html-links.description: Add a single link, then build a menu of links to connect pages.

html-images.name: Add Pictures to Your Page 🖼️
html-images.description: Make your page more interesting by adding images.

html-upload-image.name: Upload Your Own Picture 📸
html-upload-image.description: Skip internet pictures — upload a photo from your computer right into your webpage.

html-lists.name: Pack Your List 🎒
html-lists.description: Create a list of things, just like packing items for a trip.

html-tables.name: Show Data in Tables 📊
html-tables.description: Arrange information neatly using rows and columns.

html-forms.name: Build a Contact Form 📝
html-forms.description: Let people interact with your page using input fields and a button.

html-save-page.name: Save Your Webpage 💾
html-save-page.description: Save your webpage as a real .html file you can come back to anytime.

html-comments.name: Add Notes to Your Code 💬
html-comments.description: Use HTML comments to leave notes for yourself that the browser ignores.

html-sections.name: Design Your Website 🏗️
html-sections.description: Use semantic header, nav, section, and footer to design a real website layout.

html-link-pages.name: Link Pages Together 🔗
html-link-pages.description: Build a real multi-page website — Home, About, Contact — with menus that actually work.

html-divs.name: Group Things Together 🧩
html-divs.description: Use invisible containers to group parts of your page.

html-css-intro.name: Colour Your Page 🎨
html-css-intro.description: Install CSS and add your first colour and background to a heading.

html-css-fonts.name: Style Your Text ✏️
html-css-fonts.description: Change font size, alignment, and font family with CSS.

html-css-box.name: Build Beautiful Boxes 📦
html-css-box.description: Padding, border, and margin — the three layers of every element.

html-css-flex.name: Arrange Things in a Row 📐
html-css-flex.description: Use display flex to arrange cards side by side.

html-make-it-yours.name: Build Your Personal Page 🌟
html-make-it-yours.description: Combine HTML and CSS to build your own personal page with hero and button.

html-make-it-happen.name: Build It. Code It. Make It Happen 🚀
html-make-it-happen.description: Capstone project — a full styled portfolio with navigation, hero, features, and footer.

python-hello-world.name: Hello, Python! 🐍
python-hello-world.description: Write your first two lines of Python — make the penguin speak and print to the terminal

python-use-a-variable.name: Use a Variable
python-use-a-variable.description: Store a value in a name you pick, then use that name in your code

python-ask-the-user.name: Ask the User
python-ask-the-user.description: Use input to ask a question and work with the kid's answer

python-numbers.name: Numbers in Python
python-numbers.description: Do maths with Python — add, subtract, multiply, divide

python-type-conversion-fix-the-input.name: Type Conversion — Fix the Input
python-type-conversion-fix-the-input.description: Turn text into numbers and back again — int, float, str — so keyboard input can do maths

python-make-a-choice.name: Make a Choice
python-make-a-choice.description: Use if and else to make your program pick between options

python-boolean-logic-smart-decisions.name: Boolean Logic
python-boolean-logic-smart-decisions.description: Use True, False, and, or, not to build smart decisions — check two things at once, flip answers

python-guess-the-number.name: Guess the Secret Number
python-guess-the-number.description: Build a number-guessing game — the computer picks a secret, you try to find it

python-second-chance.name: Second Chance
python-second-chance.description: Use a loop to let the kid try again when they get something wrong

python-keep-trying.name: Keep Trying Till You Win
python-keep-trying.description: Use a loop that keeps running until the kid finally gets the right answer

python-break-stop-the-loop.name: Break — Stop the Loop
python-break-stop-the-loop.description: Pop out of any loop the moment something specific happens — keep going until the kid says stop

python-smart-shopping-bill.name: Smart Shopping Bill
python-smart-shopping-bill.description: Add up shopping items in a loop and print a neat bill with the total

python-more-items-bigger-bill.name: More Items, Bigger Bill
python-more-items-bigger-bill.description: Store many items in a list and loop through them to build a growing bill

python-lists-many-things-one-name.name: Lists — Many Things, One Name
python-lists-many-things-one-name.description: One name, many values in order — make a list, count it, grow it, loop through every item

python-built-in-functions-power-tools.name: Built-in Functions — Power Tools
python-built-in-functions-power-tools.description: Meet Python's ready-made commands — len, max, min, sum, sorted — the power tools you'll reach for every day

python-strings-control-your-text.name: Strings — Control Your Text
python-strings-control-your-text.description: Shape and clean up any piece of text with Python's string tools — upper, lower, split, replace, strip

python-fstrings-cleaner-text.name: F-Strings — Cleaner Text
python-fstrings-cleaner-text.description: Drop variables straight into a string with f-quote and curly braces — the way real Python prints

python-indexing-and-slicing.name: Reach Inside — Indexing and Slicing
python-indexing-and-slicing.description: Pull out one item or a chunk by position — works the same way on lists and on text

python-beat-the-computer.name: Can You Beat the Computer?
python-beat-the-computer.description: Play rock-paper-scissors against the computer and see who wins

python-tuples-fixed-together.name: Tuples — Fixed Together
python-tuples-fixed-together.description: Hold a few things together that should never change — like coordinates or an RGB colour

python-math-module-power-tools.name: Math Module
python-math-module-power-tools.description: Unlock pi, square root, powers, and rounding with Python's built-in math module

python-date-and-time.name: Date and Time
python-date-and-time.description: Get the current date and time with Python's datetime module — pull out the year, month, day, format it nicely

python-custom-commands.name: Create Your Own Commands
python-custom-commands.description: Teach Python your own commands by writing a function — use them again and again

python-one-command-multiple-answers.name: One Command, Multiple Answers
python-one-command-multiple-answers.description: Write a command that returns different answers depending on what you give it

python-connect-your-commands.name: Connect Your Commands
python-connect-your-commands.description: Call one command from inside another — connect small pieces into bigger programs

python-one-command-one-job.name: One Command, One Job
python-one-command-one-job.description: Keep each function focused on a single job — small, clear, reusable

python-handle-any-number-of-inputs.name: Handle Any Number of Inputs
python-handle-any-number-of-inputs.description: Write one command that works with any number of inputs — one, five, or fifty

python-built-in-advanced-extra-power.name: Built-in Advanced — Extra Power
python-built-in-advanced-extra-power.description: Turn long loops into one clean line with zip, enumerate, map, filter, any, all

python-pick-what-matters.name: Count What Matters
python-pick-what-matters.description: Count items in a list that match a rule — bigger than X, longer than Y, that kind of question

python-store-data-with-meaning.name: Store Data with Meaning
python-store-data-with-meaning.description: Label every piece of data with a name so you can look it up by meaning, not position

python-sets-no-duplicates.name: Sets — No Duplicates
python-sets-no-duplicates.description: A smart collection that throws out duplicates automatically — one of each, no repeats

python-try-except-handle-errors.name: Try / Except — Handle Errors
python-try-except-handle-errors.description: Catch mistakes before they crash your program — try something, and handle it if it goes wrong

python-save-your-data.name: Save Your Data
python-save-your-data.description: Build a save-and-load app with functions, files, and a menu loop — bring it all together

python-keep-it-after-restart.name: Keep It After Restart — Files
python-keep-it-after-restart.description: A cleaner way to handle files using the with keyword — automatic open and close in one block

python-build-a-list-as-you-go.name: Build a List as You Go
python-build-a-list-as-you-go.description: Collect items in a list as a loop runs and print them all at the end
